features.summary: Caterpillar reported Q1 2022 adjusted EPS of $2.88 versus $2.87 in Q1 2021, with sales increasing 14% to $13.6 billion driven by higher volumes and favorable pricing. All three primary segments achieved double-digit sales growth despite ongoing supply chain constraints, particularly semiconductors and other components. The company experienced strong end-user demand for the fifth consecutive quarter, with backlog increasing $3.4 billion. Operating profit increased 2% to $1.9 billion, though margins compressed to 13.7% due to elevated manufacturing costs from materials and freight that outpaced price realization in the quarter.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strong end-user demand and anticipates achieving Investor Day targets for adjusted operating profit margins and ME&T free cash flow of $4-8 billion for 2022. They project price increases will more than offset manufacturing cost increases for the full year, with margins expected to improve in the second half as pricing actions accelerate and cost comparisons ease. Supply chain challenges are expected to persist into Q2, though the company remains well-positioned to capture demand when constraints ease. Second quarter sales are expected to increase compared to Q1 with similar margins to the prior year.
features.risks: Primary risks include ongoing supply chain constraints limiting the company's ability to fully meet strong demand, elevated manufacturing costs from materials and freight, and COVID-19 related shutdowns in China impacting operations. Geopolitical tensions from the Russia-Ukraine conflict pose additional challenges, with the company suspending Russian manufacturing operations. Inflationary pressures on input costs may exceed current expectations, and China's construction market weakness could persist given difficult prior year comparisons and residential construction headwinds.
features.key_drivers: Strong commodity prices are driving increased utilization and investment across mining and energy sectors, with robust order growth in Energy & Transportation, particularly for reciprocating engines and solar turbines. North American construction remains supported by strong residential activity and improving non-residential demand, while infrastructure investment is expected to accelerate. The company's autonomous solutions are gaining traction with increased customer interest, and services revenue continues to grow as part of the strategy to double services sales between 2016-2026.
